meaning: n.马戏团的老板,玩杂耍的人
examples: Works on paper, objects for stage and selected parts from television programmes of this movie reflect the artist's showman qualities.
His product launches, at which he would stand alone on a black stage and conjure up an "incredible" new electronic gadget in front of an awed crowd, were the performances of a master showman.
He is a natural showman with the talents of a televangelist.
The Walt Disney comparison is particularly apt: popular showman and control freak, brilliant but menacing.
In January 1803 the anatomist and electrical showman Giovanni Aldini shocked London with a ghoulish experiment.
